criteria.name: Daily living activities
criteria.summary: The claimant must show they have difficulty with daily living activities such as preparing food, washing, dressing, communicating, reading, managing medication, or making decisions about money.
descriptors: Managing therapy or monitoring a health condition
Reading and understanding signs, symbols and words
Engaging with other people face to face

thresholds.standardRate: Score of 8 to 11 points across all daily living activities
thresholds.enhancedRate: Score of 12 or more points across all daily living activities

criteria.reference: Social Security (Personal Independence Payment) Regulations 2013, Schedule 1, Part 2

criteria.name: Mobility activities
criteria.summary: The claimant must show they have difficulty with planning and following a journey, or moving around. This covers both physical mobility and cognitive or psychological barriers to travel.

thresholds.standardRate: Score of 8 to 11 points across mobility activities
thresholds.enhancedRate: Score of 12 or more points across mobility activities

criteria.reference: Social Security (Personal Independence Payment) Regulations 2013, Schedule 1, Part 3

criteria.name: Limited capability for work
criteria.summary: The claimant must show that their physical or mental health condition limits their capability for work. This is assessed through a work capability assessment looking at specific activities.
descriptors: Absence or loss of control whilst conscious
Appropriateness of behaviour with other people

thresholds.limitedCapability: Score of 15 or more points across all activities

criteria.reference: Employment and Support Allowance Regulations 2008, Schedule 2

criteria.name: Limited capability for work-related activity
criteria.summary: The claimant must show that their condition is so severe that it would be unreasonable to require them to undertake any work-related activity. This places them in the support group.
descriptors: Cannot mobilise more than 50 metres without stopping
Cannot raise either arm to head height
Cannot press a button with either hand
Has substantial risk to themselves or others if found fit for work
Is receiving or recovering from treatment for cancer
Has severe uncontrolled episodes of loss of consciousness

thresholds.supportGroup: Meeting at least one descriptor from the support group activities

criteria.reference: Employment and Support Allowance Regulations 2008, Schedule 3

policies.benefitType: Universal Credit — Limited capability for work

criteria.name: Limited capability for work
criteria.summary: The claimant must show that their physical or mental health condition limits their capability for work. The assessment uses the same work capability assessment descriptors as ESA.
descriptors: Same descriptors as ESA limited capability for work

thresholds.limitedCapability: Score of 15 or more points across all activities

criteria.name: Limited capability for work-related activity
criteria.summary: The claimant must show that their condition is so severe that it would be unreasonable to require them to undertake any work-related activity. This results in an additional element in their Universal Credit payment.
descriptors: Same descriptors as ESA support group

thresholds.supportGroup: Meeting at least one descriptor from the support group activities

policies.legislation: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992, Section 64

criteria.name: Daytime care needs
criteria.summary: The claimant must show they need frequent attention throughout the day in connection with bodily functions, or continual supervision to avoid substantial danger to themselves or others.
descriptors: Frequent attention in connection with bodily functions
Continual supervision to avoid substantial danger

thresholds.lowerRate: Meets the day condition or the night condition

criteria.reference: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992, Section 64(2)

criteria.name: Night-time care needs
criteria.summary: The claimant must show they need prolonged or repeated attention at night in connection with bodily functions, or another person to be awake at night for a prolonged period or at frequent intervals to watch over them.
descriptors: Prolonged or repeated attention at night
Watching over at night for prolonged period or frequent intervals

thresholds.higherRate: Meets both the day condition and the night condition

criteria.reference: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992, Section 64(3)
